He likes meeting with his students 1 on 1 in the writing process. Ask him what he wants in paper and he will give you what you need. The only homework for the quarter is two 2000 word and one 4000 word papers.

Bator did a really good job with making himself available for questions through e-mail or at the library/CoHo.  He was also really cool about and flexible with any problems encountered during the quarter (comp/family problems, writer's block, whatever).  Really attentive, quirky, and interesting guy

Bator's aloof-nutty professor quality promotes a comfortable atmosphere, nevertheless he is suprisingly attentive to his students and aware of the dynamics of the classroom.  He gives his students respect and individual attention.  Workload, in a nutshell: I basically wrote 3 papers for this class.

I loved Bator.  He was really quirky and has his idiosyncracies, but that's what makes him so great.  He was a harder grader at first than he came off to be, but overall works very hard to meet with everyone and help them along.  Great class.

analysis.name: Khalil Barhoum
analysis.verdict: Good teaching but expect to put in the work, and ratings have been declining
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 3 reviews so far, take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: Khalil is fun, animated, and demonstrates a genuine concern over whether his students are learning.  He is very approachable and, while the class moves quite fast, a little effort can make it tremendously valuable.  He is a linguistic genius, but is incredibly humble about it.  I would highly recommend any of his classes.

Khalil is a tough but fair man who is dedicated to teaching his students not only language but culture.  I rank him among the most valuable professors I have ever studied from.  Arabic is a hard language - and Khalil's classes are not easy - but they are eminently rewarding and worthwhile.

Khalil is an animated, concerned teacher that is always willing to answer questions. He clearly has his students in mind, and definitely has made something as seemingly incomprehensible as Arabic into a manageable class. He is particularly good at getting us to learn outside of the book.

analysis.name: Russell Berman
analysis.verdict: Highly rated with a manageable workload
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 12 reviews, decent sample but could shift with more data

review_highlights.comment: Professor Berman's Zionism and the Novel course is A LOT more work than most write2 courses, but worth it. Two oral presentations, student discussion leads , he keeps his politics out of the class and lets us debate the real issues. Papers are hard, he reads every word, gives tons of feedback. He doesn't say much--ever, talk at him to get help.

Wonderful professor and mentor. Very low-key, but consistently supportive and helpful. He's a bit of a tough conversation though, both in class and out. He makes you do the talking, so be prepared to do that!

SOOO NICE! Professor Berman is a deep guy with a really subtle approach to ideological problems, except when he's trying to provoke a response, but don't be fooled by that, he just wants students to engage.
LOVE HIM, so cool, calm and helpful, best ever!

Nobody else shows up at all the department events and talks to students. True sometimes he can be a grumpy old walrus, but if you ask him for help he can be great. He's not hard on grades and will edit your stuff pretty well if you ASK!

analysis.name: John Bender
analysis.verdict: Below average reviews. Worth checking if this fits your learning style
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 8 reviews so far, take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: One of the kindest, most knowledgeable professors. Prof Bender is super well-read and really encourages attendance at his office hours. The workload was incredibly manageable (light, even), and he is open to adjusting the course in order to improve student experiences with it. Amazing!

wow, i think this is what they have in mind when they refer to the "ivory tower." this man is a HUGE SNOB. i would rather break my arm than have him sneering at me for my intellectual inferiority ever again...if you like your self-esteem, then stay away...far away!!
can do.. do. cannot do.. teach.  what kind of people teach "english" not till class was over did i realize all is a waste of time.  "humanities" useless degree. useless classes.
He really knows his stuff. An excellent teacher, who works hard and expects you to work hard in return. I guess people don't like him much because he expects a degree of maturity.
don't take anything with this man unless you like being sneered at and made to feel intellectually inferior to him. he is a total jerk and entirely unapproachable.

review_highlights.comment: Great French accent - enthusiastic about material
Creative slides and follows the lecture outline
really takes an interest in students

review_highlights.comment: Prof Block is brilliant and very clear, but lacks empathy and a moral core.  He seems driven to act in a way that is best described as evil.  Stay out of this man's orbit.

I'm not fond of this rating system; although Block wasn't necessarily "crystal clear" or "extremely helpful," he was still awesome! He was so enthusiastic about the subject and thrilled with teaching it to us! I don't know how he would be with more dry material (we studied Mythbusters and the scientific process), but he was great for this class.
a nice guy, expects alot, just make sure you've thought about your question seriously before you go asking for help.
Evil.  Very harsh and demoralizing.

analysis.name: Chris Bobonich
analysis.verdict: Well liked with reasonable difficulty, and ratings have been declining
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 7 reviews so far, take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: Very entertaining, and he teaches the material very well, too.  His lectures tend to include some kind of example or thought experiment that results in the gruesome death of an unsuspecting student.

I have to admit that I really enjoyed this IHUM. It's easily the best winter/spring IHUM. He is crazy yet brilliant, clear, and very entertaining. The reading was very interesting and the course tied together well. I very highly recommend both Bobonich as a prof and the Fate of Reason as an IHUM in general.

uses very unusual & funny examples to illustrate moral dilemmas. like candy bars laced with arsenic. very kooky, super engaging, walks down the aisles while speaking, hardly ever just "reads" from the notes. every lecture's like a miniature performance.  pretty awesome.
Bobonich is crazy but extremely clear and good at making complex ideas simple. Going to lecture is like going to a play. He's amazing...a gem.

Very entertaining in class- uses all sorts of crazy analogies and hypothetical situations about chandeliers falling on people and violence involving maces, shotguns, and burning oil. He is also a vegan, which is cool because it means he takes his philosophy seriously.

analysis.name: Stephen Boyd
analysis.verdict: Good teaching but expect to put in the work
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 14 reviews, decent sample but could shift with more data

review_highlights.comment: Always elaborate on the really easy points, and skip the difficult ones that most need explaining. Rather read lecture notes than going to lectures.

Super awesome professor.  Is able to given intuitive interpretations to very complicated mathematical concepts.  Gives good explanation of the theory, and focuses on how to apply it to real world problems.  I feel priveledged to have been able to take this course from this professor.

I've taken EE263, 363, and 364 from Boyd. Probably the best teacher I've had at Stanford. The classes are all quite applied, in my opinion; there is theory but it's always brought back down to earth with plenty of scientific computing. Just an outstanding teacher whose problem sets will let you master some challenging material.

I think that 263 has (almost) reached perfection. Great material and useful and enjoyable class. I don't know if his behavior is arrogant or if it's related to his sense of humour (which I like). He's also very witty. I think that it would be a pity to leave EE and not have taken a class from Prof. Boyd.

A lot of reviews have mentioned that Boyd is full of himself--I don't get that impression at all.  He does like to rag on other fields, but most of his criticism is warranted.  He has a slightly cheesy sense of humor, knows the subject well, and tries to keep the lectures entertaining.  HWs are long but not bad if you're a math person.

analysis.name: Jo Boaler
analysis.verdict: Good teaching but expect to put in the work, and ratings have been declining
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 3 reviews so far, take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: Prof. Jo Boaler's class has been the best Education methodology course that I have taken at Stanford. She presents her ideas in a clear manner and really pushes her students to critically examine what we take for granted as researchers. Even if you have no interest in math, I think any budding researcher can benefit from taking this course with Jo.

Jo chose incredibly helpful readings for this course. I learned important fundamentals about research in math education from this class. We also had interesting and insightful discussions, and the research project and paper we had to do for the class were very very useful.

Professor Boaler had a fast-paced, challenging course. She really kept it interesting and we did a variety of exercises and we had a variety of assessments. She was available outside of class, as well for consultation on our projects and papers.

analysis.name: Brett Bourbon
analysis.verdict: Good teaching but expect to put in the work, and ratings have been declining

review_highlights.comment: Brett was a champ. I ended up taking a four or more classes with him.  By focusing on what literature can tell us about the meaning of a good life and so forth, Brett's classes are less English literature, and more philosophy of literature, philosophy of mind, trending towards linguistics and neuroscience.  Highly, highly recommended.
Brett is a genius.  His class is poorly structured at times, but you should take it just for the offhand insights he'll throw at you.  I can't stop thinking about what he talks about in class.
actually, he teaches at the University of Dallas now and he is great. there is a lack of structure however, his philosophical based lit classes are awesome!
Don't be put off by the t-shirts or the rapidly recited abstractions.  He makes every other English class seem boring and pretentious and empty of meaning.
Brett is a real gem on the Stanford Campus. Don't leave without a class with him.

He also invites every student to have lunch with him once the semester. One negative thing about his class is that it is quite easy and light on math....
Boskin doesn't so much teach Econ 1 as tell the class about his personal biases in economics.  He is a very smart guy and he knows the field, but not great at telling the students about econ.
He knows what he's talking about...think Bush's economic policy advisor, but his Econ1B class is pretty boring. He's a nice guy, though, and loves talking to students.
Boskin is one of the least compelling professors I have ever come across. He is exceptionally arrogant and it was clear that he wished he was somewhere else.
I really liked Boskin... He was conservative, but it was a nice change.  He knows his stuff, has interesting stories, and seemed excited about teaching.

analysis.name: Bruce Lusignan
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews. Student experiences vary quite a bit, and ratings have been declining

review_highlights.comment: I attended Bruce's Systems Engineering Classes in 1968 ansd 1969 and it was the best course I ever did ever (graduate of Cambridge in agriculture, post grad economics, project evaluation and stats). His approach is what I have used during the whole of my professional life as a consultant working for leading agencies... and still going strong.
I disagree, Bruce Lusignan is willing to go to considerable lengths to help his students, truly a caring professor amidst self-absorbed colleagues who could care less about a student.

We were all post-grads with some Prima Donnas who quite soon dropped out - they could not handle situations where others had better solutions. Lusignan graded based on the degree students worked through from concepts to useful results. The talkers and show folk with bright ideas but no delivery did not do well. Best course I ever did!!

I would advise against taking any classes with Bruce.  He has only the most limited understanding of course concepts, and when someone points out that he's done something wrong, he often says that students are "too stupid" to understand the material.

Bruce is a flaming liberal.  Be prepared to get a low grade if you try to make a conservative argument.  This class is known as an easy A for jocks, but I got a B since I tried to propose social security reform.  Unbelievable.

analysis.name: Brajesh Samarth

review_highlights.comment: Truly amazing teacher. Incorporates the culture, history, and literature of India with language learning. Listens to each student, facilitates just what is needed for comprehension (but lets you get there yourself), is unfailingly supportive, and has a great laugh too.

Really awesome professor. Tries to make a community out of the classroom. He listens to students' issues/requests and actually responds to them in how he chooses to structure the class. Laidback but also pushes everyone in their language ability.

I had this professor a number of years ago. He contextualized Hindi within its social, cultural and political contexts. He makes you feel at ease speaking, which is especially important for beginning-level language learners.
I am taking class with Dr. Brajesh at Emory University right now. Simply the best professor. He cares so much about his students and so friendly.I would love to take his class again.
Graduate students beware: This lecturer definitely throws an undergraduate pitch. It's more like a student social club than a class. Nice enough but doesn't really teach grammar.

No assigned pieces to look at before class, basically no syllabus.  Don't waste your time with the class, just go read his books.

scott is the best teacher i have ever had in my entire life and i seriously regret not majoring in film or taking more than 1 class with him

runs hot, runs cold. what's up? who knows...

so frustratingly bad- horrible time management- blabs on and on

She's so unorganized. She didn't give us any grades, until the end of the quarter when you suddenly get a grade with no explanation.  She only gave feedback on one of the four projects for the class ( and even that didn't get a grade).

He is a very clear lecturer and very patient.

A competent and caring professor, Charlie loves his material and it shows. Work hard, don't be afraid to ask him any questions (and you will have questions, chemistry is full of complexities and exceptions) and you'll do great!
Incredible teacher. So dedicated. Really goes out of his way to help students.

Analytical chem is not them most interesting subject and with the writing in the major added on top of that, this course could have been a nightmare. Dr. Cox was very helpful and was easy to follow in lecture. I felt that the grading was extremely harsh but you get revisions. Compared to other chem courses this one is tough but has lots of support.

Not too bad. This year he was manageable but outreach and the workbook proved to be very valuable. In fact, it was almost required to go to the Friday outreach to get through the course--especially the first part. Cox taught the acid/base and inorganic stuff. Fair exams--similar to the workbook with some conceptual twists. I would take him again.

Very arrogant. Loved to make things more complicated than they needed to be. Thought the whole Mecahnical Engineering curriculum should be centered around thermodynamics. I have been working in industry for several years and that is the subject area that I use least from the core courses.

The class is too much work. You do learn a lot though and in the end it is worth it. I liked it. This must be taken with a grain of salt because I also enjoy dressing up in latex and being whipped. He is not a bad lecturer. Unfortunately he does not lecture on everything you need to know to complete the lab reports, the rationale being that you nee

really funny, nice teacher; class was random and all over the place. not very useful or impressive...dum class, aight proff

History 233F - Political Thought in Early Modern Britain - was an expertly-facilitated 3-student colloquium in which we read the great works of British political philosophy, including the Leviathan, and the 2nd Treatise of Government.  Dr. Como treated the course as a discussion group supplemented with brief lectures.  I learned a great deal.

the class i took was on the tudors and it was awesome. prof como is interesting and really helpful - plus the tudors are just great. the midterm was pretty easy but the final was kinda hard. but anyway prof como taught me a lot and i had fun.

It's hard to say this without sounding ridiculous, but he is honestly the best professor I have ever had. Not only that, but I'd venture to say he might be the world's premier professor when it comes to the understanding of democracy. Anyone who passes up the opportunity to take his class is doing themselves a massive disservice.
Incredible professor. Very knowledgeble and helpful. Best scholar at Stanford PS. His class is a MUST.

analysis.name: Doug McAdam

SUPER SUPER SUPER GUY! Take his class, it's absolutely amazing. He is SO FUNNY.  His grading is fair, and class is not too hard. SOC 1 is extremely interesting.
McAdam is amazing! He's an extremely fair grader and also very engaging! Take his class you won't regret it.
I love McAdams class. He's super funny but also makes you very interested in the subject. Great guy

analysis.name: Dominik Michels
analysis.verdict: Good teaching but expect to put in the work
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 7 reviews so far, take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: Amazing Professor. Very clear and consistent. Lectures are extremely interesting.
Very well-organized class on hard topics. Great lecture notes! Freedom to work on a final project. Fair grading.
A lot of work, but perfect internship. :))
Great professor, challenging class.

analysis.name: Dustin Schroeder
analysis.verdict: Well liked with reasonable difficulty
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 3 reviews so far, take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: Amazing teacher who loves to get to know his students. His enthusiasm and excitement are contagious and I found myself devoting time to his assignments because I enjoyed them more than because I needed to. Grades very high and gives students lots of freedom with their assignments.

Absolutely amazing professor and class. He's super great at breaking down concepts and explaining things. It's and introsem so it'll be small class, but that's great because he's definitely a professor who works well with all his students. The subject is super interesting, and he manages to blend so many aspects of it together perfectly.
Really cares about his students, reasonable expectations, cooked us all an amazing dinner too

analysis.name: Douglas Osheroff
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews. Student experiences vary quite a bit, and ratings have been declining
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 19 reviews, decent sample but could shift with more data

review_highlights.comment: Notes are incomprehensible. Did not make sense in any of the lectures at all. The class only made sense once I did my problem sets, otherwise was completely useless. Has a strange sense of humor though, has a flair for being theatrical and goofy. The final was suprisingly easy, said it was his "christmas present" for us.

he's a grandfatherly professor who means well, but he's totally incomprehensible and his lecture is just babbling/regurgitating lecture notes. at least for me, it was really rough, plus 43/e&m is the toughest of the 40s series... i'd really avoid him if you can
Shows a lot of concern for students if you approach him, though that's made rather difficult by the horde of students fawning over him during office hours... dare I call them Nobel groupies?
Really nice.  His enthusiasm in lecture makes physics actually bearable.  He loves doing demos and gives u lecture notes every class, huge bonus.
His lecture notes are kind of illegible and his lecture can wander off. Likes to mention his award in class a lot. Approachable, though.

analysis.name: Dylan McKay
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews. Student experiences vary quite a bit, and ratings have been declining
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 4 reviews so far, take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: I'm not a Stanford student so I can't compare this to previous iterations. However, I felt like Dylan was an excellent teacher and turned discrete math into a topic that was very engaging. We had great conversations and he even emailed me once to see if I was doing okay. So he's a genuine, great guy. Also very funny.
CS103 instructor. Great class. Problem sets were actually really interesting towards the end of the course.

Dylan is the kinda guy that makes you excited to come to class every day. His excitement, kindness, and passion make his class fun and interesting. He reminded me why I got into CS in the first place and I can't wait to take more classes with him in the future.
He is nice, but the course CS 103 is SO DIFFICULT!!!

analysis.name: Liran Einav
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews. Student experiences vary quite a bit, and ratings have been declining
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 7 reviews so far, take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: Absolutely painful experience, it is so hard to understand him even though you know that he cares deeply about his students. Don't even bother asking him questions though, even though he cares about students, he thinks you should know it already, so he will dismiss your questions. The psets take forever, overshadow other class. Tests are hard

I disagree with the other ratings, I really liked Einav. Yes, he's foreign and the material can get tricky; he is also not the most dynamic lecturer. But he is very clear and methodical. The problem sets and exams are well chosen; they test knowledge while also challenging us to go above and beyond. I'd recommend this class.
So uncomprehensible that I had to drop the class and take it later; so unpersonable that I didn't feel badly at all about doing it.
Don't take this class from Einav.  Thick accent makes him hard to understand.  Problem sets are very hard and graded very harshly.

analysis.name: Justin Eichenlaub
analysis.verdict: Good teaching but expect to put in the work, but ratings have been improving recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 8 reviews so far, take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: Really nice and clearly understands what he teaches. Tends to respond to students with an "ok cool" rather than providing feedback and facilitating discussion..which was annoying. Readings could be long but they were always interesting and overall I enjoyed the class. He just needs more experience, he looks like my older brother, not a professor.

Justin is a good teacher but prefers to discuss rather than really lecture... made it a bit difficult to take notes. There were just two exams and one really needs to read and remember the key points of all the assigned readings to do well on these exams. There was also one 5-page paper of your choice due which was pretty simple.

My favorite professor this semester! He's clearly passionate about the material, and had a great time conveying it to us. For once, I found class discussions to be both interesting and helpful, instead of boring and/or useless. Very fun and personable - I had a great time in his class, and learned a lot!

This was an AWESOME class. He's a great teacher. So smart and takes an interest in the students. You want to work hard for him because he makes the class super interesting. Do the readings. Hes very helpful and a fair grader. Highly suggest this class!
Wonderful course. Fantastic discussion leader, really listens to students and takes time to explain challenging ideas. I would definitely recommend his class!

analysis.name: Ewart Thomas
analysis.verdict: Well liked with reasonable difficulty, and ratings have been declining
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 13 reviews, decent sample but could shift with more data

review_highlights.comment: If you need to take Stats, be sure and take it with Prof.Thomas. He explains concepts well, his exams are straight forward and follow his course notes, and he is very helpful during his office hours. Don't be afraid to get help from him if you're finding things confusing. He is a great teacher.

Prof Thomas is one of the best professors I have had at Stanford. If you apply yourself *a little*, you will lean a *whole lot* from him. What I like most about his classes, both the undergrad and the grad, is  that you learn how to practically apply the skills that you learn.
Don't buy the book. His handouts are awesome and quite legendary. He makes things simple and easy to understand. :)  Definately take stats with this guy no matter what!
Prof. Ewart is very passionate about Stats.  You will learn a lot from him.
Super unclear lecturer and even more unclear assignments.

Go to see him in his office for help on hw and final and you will do well AND learn a lot.

Seems like a nice guy but his teaching style was not for me. Totally hands off, no examples, does not really allow students to ask questions. It's all about his powerpoint. If you dont get it, too bad.
Decent lecturer, but discourages asking questions and isn't not very open to interacting with students

Hard class. All the Math 50 series profs are hard but Math 52 is especially hard. They seem to be geniuses who don't understand that we're newbies trying to learn what they've already understood a million times over.

He is gorgeous. Don't believe me, check out his personal website (linked to the math department's). His lectures were utterly incomprehensable during class, but if I wrote down everything he said, they were very helpful when I read them over (after reading the book). His accent was also very difficult to understand.
Heavy accent, yet writing is very clear, notes are extremely useful.
Nice guy , uses AT THE END OF THE DAY alot ; you can have fun too

Created stupid busy work and had a condescending attitude, on top of all of other short comings.

He had no logical order in his lectures, and what he taught was pointless and erratic.  And yes, he shrieked in class.  Like a banshee.  (The rest of the course, Lit into Life, was quite enjoyable, however.)

This guy's lectures seem like they're on the verge of being brilliant, but never really quite make it.  You feel like he could be the most interesting person in the world if he only wanted to be.  Sings badly and often.  Has focus issues.  The material of the class holds its own, though, despite the strange lectures.
Prof. Felstiner made me laugh every single lecture.  He always had really interesting things to say and he made lecture highly entertaining!

Ken's "rambling lectures" are not only captivatingly interesting, but also an inspiring example for how we ought to think- always tangentially, never linearly. LIFE CHANGINGLY FUN!
Ken is and incredible, one-of-a-kind professor who udnerstand the deeper implications of literary works. Anybody who thinks he is boring should probably be majoring in investment banking.  Have fun.

On the one hand, he's not a bad guy. Decent grader. On the other, he dwells rather incessantly on his former problems with the bottle, which he interrupts otherwise productive discussions to expound to the class. Also feels the need to share with us the writings of all his close friends.
Good poet with an amazing archive of quotations in his head.  Lectures have little substance though, and facilitates discussion badly with "Well, what do you think?"
One of the lamest professors I ever had.  Every class he yammers on about his personal problems and how they related to whatever book we read.  Also a tough grader.  Steer clear.

Extremely knowledgable, but one of the most boring teachers I've ever had. I was in very small class (only 3 people), but he just spent the whole time lecturing in a monotone.
Excellent teacher. Unbelievable breadth of knowledge. Accessible. Passion for topic.

The worst course in my educational career.
Best professor I ever had at Stanford, in my honest opinion. Lots of creative ways of teaching which sparks intelligent minds to really think and engage.
lack of textbook makes it a little hard and he's sometimes difficult to follow, but he's willing to help and the grades are not hard to come by.
Practical with much hands on experience. An inspirational course which motivates independent thinking.
extremely knowledgable with highly conductive reasoning.  Highly recommend for any engineering major.

Practical, passionate, probably the best in my 4 years of undergrad.
This guy is awesome!!  he knows his stuff, has very helpful photos of everything imaginable.  He also has one heck of a story to tell and is a comedian to boot.
Best prof ever. He's an amazing teacher and an even better academic advisor. ;-) If you think you might be an AA major/minor definitely ask him to advise you.

analysis.name: Giorgio Gratta

Thankfully most people taking this class are doing it for a requirement so it was easy to stay above the curve.

Unclear everything - subject materials, tests, and grading. I got a B+ with no real explanation, even when I scored well above the median on tests. Did not learn a thing from his lectures. So, so terrible.

What a joke. His teaching is so bad that maybe 20% of the class attended lecture after the first week. Lectures were dry and the exams looked nothing like we were taught. The class averages were also dismal, but atleast he made the curve high to offset his garbage teaching skills.
Just okay - not great, not bad either.  Made physics 45 pretty easy.

analysis.name: Giovanni Tempesta

If you want to take a class that you will truly love... TAKE THIS PROFESSOR! He is the coolest teacher in the entire university. Your a fool if you don't have have the privlage of taking a class with him!
Professore Tempesta is an amazing teacher, incredibly kind and patient. His enthusiasm for the Italian language and culture made me look forward to coming to class everyday!

Professor Tempesta is a gem. I struggled with languages but enrolled in Italian for my language credit at Stanford. He cares for his students like no other, offering to meet outside of class and getting to know everyone on personal levels. My discussion and conversational Italian skills improved tremendously and Ill be recommending him to everyone
Professor Tempesta is simply a brilliant teacher.

analysis.name: Paul Goldstein
analysis.verdict: Good teaching but expect to put in the work

review_highlights.comment: Wow!  An outlook and approach that synthesizes east coast intensity with west coast cool.  One of my favorites in law school and an inspiration for my career.  Made class a pleasure and made the profession of being law professor look like the challenge and joy that it is.
one of the most clear, organized lecturers at SLS.  very engaging and makes the material interesting.
A very talented and inspiring professor. One of my favorites at SLS.

analysis.name: James Gross
analysis.verdict: Highly rated with a manageable workload, and ratings have been declining

Although, the book is kind of a pain to get through, Prof. Gross is worth it. I've heard the other two Psych1 professors guest lecture and Gross is definitely the best out of the 3. Take this class!!

This guy just loves himself.  If you ask him about anything that he's not directly interested in (ie memory, devo, social) he turns it back onto himself or tells you it's unimportant.  Going to class is like watching him intellectually ****--funny in the abstract, miserable in practice.

Professor Gross is one of the greatest professors I've had at Stanford. He is an extremely enthusiastic and knowledgeable professor who cares about his students and has a wonderful sense of humor. His class made me wish I had majored in psychology!

Awesome teacher! Always funny and entertaining. Book is important for picky exam questions and there are always a few questions from lecture. So do both, but lecture is fun so you won't mind. ^^ Class easy to get an A in tho.

one of the best professors ever. i was never bored during his lectures b/c he always made everything really entertaining and funny! i highly recommend taking any class he teaches, especially psych 101

analysis.name: Grant Rozeboom
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews. Student experiences vary quite a bit, and ratings have been declining
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 4 reviews so far, take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: When discussing Satz's theory of "Noxious Markets," the professor chose as an example "a market no one could find noxious, like bananas." When a student reminded him about Guatemala and United Fruit he changed the subject. That same class, he made a joke about Banana Republic being his favorite store. No irony, no awareness of history, just myopia.

Professor Rozeboom has been my favorite teacher in college. Work hard and get an A. I didn't care about ethics before this class but I truly enjoyed each and every class. HIs clarity is amazing and he may be the definition of a genius. Truly a pleasure being in his class.

It's an ethics class and there is no discussion of core philosophers on the subject, such as Nietzsche and Foucault, instead focusing on sophomoric utilitarians like Nozick and Rawls. Validates dominant cultures while ignoring any theories by people of color. Laden in normative theory with neither space for emancipatory thought nor postmodernism.
Professor Rozeboom is awesome! His class challenged me to think harder than I ever had. Show up, do the work, and don't forget to participate!

analysis.name: Sepp Gumbrecht
analysis.verdict: Well liked with reasonable difficulty, and ratings have been declining

review_highlights.comment: Just great, kind, helpful, a world citizen open to new ideas. An acquired taste and definitely requires listening stamina, but well worth it.
A fantastic conversationalist and wonderfully generous and genius teacher. My favorite one of all time. But I don't like football.
great brilliant helpful charming teacher

analysis.name: Heather Hadlock

I agree. The article was very illuminating. I can't say it added to my knowledge of music but it certainly improved my sex life! And isn't that what college is for?
Excellent professor and mentor.

analysis.name: Henry Lowood

Do you like condescending instructors? Instructors who think their class is the only one you are taking? Lots of reading that has nothing to do with assignments? Understands that his time is precious, but your time means nothing? The feeling that you've read a lot/done lots of work but learned very little? Then this guy is for you!

Interesting lectures and Lowood was surprisingly funny, occasionally dry but overall an enjoyable class. Some weeks have a lot of reading but as long as you keep up and go to lecture you'll be more than fine.
Meh. Wasn't my cup of tea. He had pretty interesting content though, so if you like digital archiving, then consider it. But he was a little confusing and unclear on his expectations of the class.

I took his History of games and he was very enthusiastic and happy to share all his knowledge, by far the best professor iv'e had as of yet, If I can take more classes with him i'll do it in a heartbeat.

analysis.name: Scott Herdon

He is brilliant!!!! Amazing  person and professor. I learned a lot from his class and he was always very very helpful. I hope I can meet with him again in the future. Advice: meet him!
He is a great professor. One of the best! I learned a lot from his class, and I wish I can take the class again in the future with him. He is brilliant and taught me a lot!!
Herndon is brilliant and cares deeply about his students' success. He is what all teachers should be--engaged, kind, and wonderfully intelligent.
Great professor! I had a lot of fun in this class. He keeps the class very interesting and it is not outrageously hard.

Robert makes u. singers a lot of fun!
University Singers is tons of fun.

review_highlights.comment: To get this out of the way: I dare you find a more handsome professor at Stanford - not likely. Think Harrison Ford without the ruggedness. The powerful rhetoric, the persuasive arguments, the kindness, the gentle humor, the courage, the authenticity. I'm in love.
One of my favorite and most impactful professors -- I rarely take notes and found myself hastily recording his poetic words and profound insights.

analysis.name: Wray Huestis

She's pretty clear about things and teaches with a straight-forward style. Boring at times though. Really boring at times...
Best teacher I've ever had in high school, college, graduate school, and medical school. She is simply awesome!
She is extremely helpful. Her course reader is the most useful chemistry book ever. She is very matronly.

Worst class I've taken in the History Department. Lectures are unclear, rambling, and frustratingly pointless. Do yourself a favor and avoid her at all costs. There are too many great courses at Stanford to waste any time in her classes.
Great teacher! 5/5 would advise a friend.

analysis.name: Jeannie Lythcott

Great prof with loads of insightful info. Models the type of teacher we hope to become. Sense of humor.

review_highlights.comment: I found much of this class a waste of my time.  We are asked to read about a dozen different theories of adolescent development and force fit them to a case study student. Friendly and enthusiastic, but very confusing in lecture - I understood some material prior to lecture and left confused. I took to reading my textbook in class instead of listening, which served me surprisingly well.

review_highlights.comment: Mark is a nice guy and is clearer than many professors, but his teaching style is terrible.
